data.title: Elektros says U.S. grants patent for multi-port EV charging system
data.description:
• Elektros (ELEK) on Wednesday said that the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office granted it U.S. Patent No. 12,522,100 B1 covering a multi-port charging assembly for electric vehicles.
  • The company said the patented system is designed to combine multiple independent power inputs through a single charging interface, aiming to reduce electric vehicle charging times while remaining compatible with existing vehicle standards.

  • Elektros said the patent, issued on January 13, 2026, includes 20 claims and carries a 713-day patent term extension under U.S. law.

  • The invention was developed by Chief Executive Shlomo Bleier and is assigned to the company.

  • The company said the technology could be applied across passenger, commercial and specialty electric vehicle platforms.
  • ELEK closed +45.0% at $0.0087.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Moderna gains as Arbutus/ Genevant settlement removes major financial risk
data.description:
Moderna (MRNA) shares continued to rise in the premarket on Wednesday as analysts argued that the company’s agreement to resolve a patent dispute with Arbutus Biopharma (ABUS) and the Roivant unit (ROIV), Genevant Sciences, removed a key legal overhang for the COVID vaccine maker.

Shares of Moderna (MRNA) rose in the post-market on Tuesday after the Cambridge, Massachusetts-based biotech agreed to pay $950M to settle a legal dispute related to the delivery technology behind its COVID-19 vaccine.

Under the agreement, Moderna (MRNA) will also appeal to the Federal Circuit to argue that it has government-contractor immunity, which limits its liability. If the court rules against the argument, Moderna (MRNA) is expected to pay up to an additional $1.3B within 90 days of that decision.

According to Bloomberg, Citi analyst Geoff Meacham, with a Neutral rating on MRNA, argued that the deal does “remove an overhang for the stock,” while it is “not the best-case scenario but better than many had feared.”

William Blair analyst Myles Minter saw “Moderna moving past this litigation overhang as a positive clearing event.” Minter, with a Market Perform rating on Moderna (MRNA), added that “the settlement value was better than feared.”

J.P. Morgan’s Brian Cheng opined that while the company has to pay up to $2.3B under the agreement, it was lower than the $5B estimated for the litigation. “Taking the case through a jury trial could, in our view, add another layer of uncertainty," Cheng wrote with an Overweight rating on Roivant (ROIV).

Cantor Fitzgerald’s Prakhar Agrawal, with a similar rating on ROIV, forecasts a very high probability for Moderna (MRNA) paying $1.3B additionally, as Section 1498, the federal statute that the company plans to appeal, “has already been decided in Genevant’s/Arbutus' favor multiple times."

data.title: Apple takes wraps off new low-cost MacBook Neo
data.description:
• Marking its third day of product announcements, Apple (AAPL) unveiled its new low-cost MacBook Neo on Wednesday.
  • The new MacBook is powered by the A18 Pro processor, which first appeared in the iPhone 16 Pro, Apple said. The MacBook Neo will start at $599 and is aimed at competing with low-cost Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) Chromebooks and the Microsoft (MSFT) Surface.
  • On Monday, Apple unveiled the iPhone 17e and an updated version of the iPad Air. That was followed up with new versions of the MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, and new Studio Displays.
  • Following the release of the MacBook Neo, the tech giant will host an “Apple Experience” in New York, London, and Shanghai this morning to show off the newly announced  electronics.

This is breaking news...

data.title: Elutia regains full Nasdaq listing compliance
data.description:
• Elutia (ELUT) has regained compliance with Nasdaq listing standards.
  • The company met the minimum bid price requirement.
  • Elutia also satisfied the minimum market value of listed securities requirement.
  • ELUT shares up 6.5% premarket.

data.title: Garden Stage to implement 1-for-200 share consolidation to meet Nasdaq rule
data.description:
• Garden Stage Limited (GSIW) said on Wednesday it will consolidate its Class A shares on a 200-for-1 basis, effective March 6, in a bid to regain compliance with Nasdaq Marketplace Rule 5550(a)(2) and maintain its listing.
  • The Hong Kong-based financial services provider said its Class A shares will continue trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “GSIW” but with a new CUSIP number, G3730L131, from the opening of trading on March 6.
  • Each 200 Class A shares will automatically convert into one Class A share with a par value of $0.02.
  • Fractional shares will be cancelled and returned to the pool of authorized but unissued shares. The consolidation will not affect the company’s Class B shares.
  • Prior to the consolidation, the company had 282,485,623 Class A shares and 9,250,000 Class B shares outstanding.
  • Following the consolidation, it expects about 1,412,428 Class A shares to be outstanding, while Class B shares will remain at 9,250,000.
  • GSIW -36.88% premarket to $0.0568.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Barclays owed ~£500M by collapsed lender MFS - report
data.description:
Companies associated with  Market Financial Solutions Ltd. owe Barclays (BCS) ~£500M ($669M), one of the largest exposures to the collapsed U.K. mortgage lender, according to a media report on Wednesday.

The amount is lower than the £600M exposure initially mentioned last week by the judge who is overseeing the insolvency case, Bloomberg News said, citing a person familiar with the matter. By comparison, Apollo Global Management's (APO) Atlas SP Partners unit is owed ~£400M, and Elliott Investment Management is owed ~£200M.

The three creditors are among a group of Wall Street companies that arranged more than £2B of loans to MFS, which filed for insolvency proceedings amid allegations of fraud, including double-pledging of assets, Bloomberg said.

data.title: Tevogen approves 1-for-50 reverse share split
data.description:
• Tevogen (NASDAQ: TVGN) announced on Wednesday that it had approved a 1-for-50 reverse stock split of its common shares, following prior shareholder approval on Feb. 19, 2026.
  • The reverse split intends to regain compliance with the $1 minimum bid requirement of the Nasdaq Global Market and is set to take effect on March 6, 2026, with shares to continue trading under TVGN.
  • Pursuant to the transaction, every 50 issued and outstanding common shares will be automatically converted into one share, with no fractional shares to be issued.
  • The total includes holdings of directors and executives, who beneficially own over 70% of the common stock, based on 201.42M shares outstanding as of Jan. 14, 2026.

data.title: Stock index futures as traders assess the latest labor report; eyes on Middle East tensions
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Wednesday as traders continued to monitor developments in the Middle East and digested the better-than-expected labor report.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) edged higher by +0.4%. Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.5%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.3%.

U.S. private sector employment grew by 63K jobs in February, topping the +43K consensus and accelerating from the 11K added in January, according to data released by ADP on Wednesday.

Oil inched lower, with crude oil futures (CL1:COM) down 1.22% to $73.65.

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1.6 basis points to 4.09%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) advanced 3.5 basis points to 3.54%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) moved up 2.4 basis points to 4.73%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included Coinbase Global (COIN) +5.86%, Ross Stores (ROST) +5.78%, and Moderna (MRNA) +4.56%. Decliners included Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) -3.68%, Gen Digital (GEN) -1.96%, and Principal Financial Group (PFG) -1.65%.
